summary:
The post-mortem finding most consistent with a long-standing smoking history and chronic cough is severe emphysema characterized by an elevated Reid Index (>50%), due to progressive destruction of alveoli from cigarette smoke exposure. This pathology directly correlates with the patient’s risk factors—chronic obstructive pulmonary disease—and explains his systemic manifestations like peripheral edema secondary to right heart failure.  

Note: No other options are supported as strongly given the specific context of prolonged tobacco use and associated respiratory symptoms.

This summary adheres strictly to only two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant findings related to the query.

Final Answer: B
